message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Auto-advance after clarify is a good addition, will update US-GTD-2">
Carson, this is a good refinement. The sequential detail-page pattern with auto-advance to the next inbox item is a nice UX touch. Let me reconcile this with what's already documented:

The user stories already specify `/inbox/{id}/clarify` as the clarify page (US-GTD-2). Your pattern adds two refinements:

1. **Auto-advance after processing**: After clarifying an item, redirect to `/inbox/{next-id}/clarify` (or "Inbox clear!" if empty). This is a great flow improvement -- the user stays in processing mode without manually clicking back to the inbox list and selecting the next item. I'll add this to the acceptance criteria.

2. **The clarify page is a dedicated detail page, not inline**: Already agreed. The consensus is a single form on its own page, not inline editing on the list view.

One clarification on the interaction model: the team consensus is that the clarify form shows all destination options at once (radio buttons: Next Action, Project, Waiting For, Someday/Maybe, Trash) rather than a multi-step yes/no -> then what? flow. So steps 3-4 in your description are collapsed into one form submission. But the auto-advance behavior (step 5) is additive and valuable.

I'll add the auto-advance criterion to US-GTD-2. Good catch.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Marty's confirming the auto-advance addition and clarifying that the single-form pattern is the consensus. I already accepted this in my previous message, so a brief acknowledgment is all that's needed.

input.content:
Marty, exactly right. The auto-advance is additive — it works the same regardless of whether the clarify form is a multi-step wizard or a single-form submission. User fills out the single form, submits, server processes, redirects to `/inbox/{next-id}/clarify`. Clean.

Thanks for adding it to US-GTD-2. That's the last piece I wanted in the acceptance criteria.
input.summary: Confirmed: auto-advance works with single-form pattern

routing.summary: Confirmed: auto-advance works with single-form pattern

content.text: Confirmed with Marty. All my feedback has been incorporated. Standing by for team-wide consensus and next steps.
